author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
pm context and pm next currently assemble their working set from structural rules (dependency-aware actionability per ADR pm-9x6x: ready = active leaf with no open blocked_by, ordered by compareCriticalItems). That is correct but shallow: it uses only a fraction of the data the tracker already records, and it does not adapt between a 10-item hobby workspace and a million-item, decades-spanning one.

Proposal: a pluggable relevance-scoring engine (SDK-first) that ranks candidate items for context assembly by combining ALL available signals:
- recency and activity density from the per-item history streams (recently touched, recently discussed, aging-WIP)
- dependency-graph proximity to the agent's focus item, active claims, and current plan steps
- structural priority signals: priority, risk, severity, deadline pressure, sprint/release membership
- semantic similarity between the agent's focus/query and item corpus (reusing the existing hybrid search providers)
- knowledge density: items carrying learnings/decisions relevant to the working set get surfaced so past lessons are re-injected instead of re-learned
- author affinity: what this agent (PM_AUTHOR) touched before

Design constraints:
- Scoring lives in the SDK as a documented, replaceable interface (scoreContextCandidates); the built-in scorer is just the default implementation — projects can ship their own via extensions.
- Deterministic and explainable: --explain-ranking (or a score breakdown in json output) shows per-signal contributions, so agents and humans can audit why an item was surfaced.
- Token-budgeted assembly: given a budget (explicit flag or config default), the engine packs the highest-value items and degrades gracefully (tighter projections before dropping items).
- O(result) at scale: signal inputs must come from the derived read index (pm-801d) and search index, never a full workspace scan; benchmark on the pm-mi2x synthetic workspaces.
- Small-workspace neutrality: on tiny workspaces the ranking must reduce to today's sensible ordering with zero configuration.

This is the core of the 'project management = context management' philosophy: the optimal context at any time, computed from all data, metadata, and history the workspace already has.
Adaptive context relevance engine: multi-signal scoring over metadata, graph, and history drives context/next assembly under an explicit token budget

SDK exposes a documented pluggable context-scoring interface with the built-in multi-signal scorer as default; pm context/pm next accept a token-budget bound and pack results by score with graceful projection degradation; json output can include a per-signal score breakdown; ranking is deterministic for a fixed workspace state; hot path stays index-backed (no full scan) and meets pm-9rxu latency targets on the 100k and 1M benchmark workspaces; on small workspaces default ordering remains stable (golden tests); extension can replace the scorer end-to-end (exemplar test)

message: Long-horizon context-quality planning: multi-signal relevance gap

author: codex-agent
message: Split adaptive context relevance engine into implementation slices

author: codex-agent

patch.value: SDK-first adaptive ranking engine for pm context/next: derive multi-signal relevance from tracker metadata, graph, history, semantic search, and agent state, then assemble bounded-token context with explainable scores.

message: Backfill concise description after splitting context engine into child slices

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Planning evidence 2026-07-07: all-status duplicate checks showed pm-4k6b is the canonical adaptive context engine parent; no focused child existed for signal extraction, scorer contract, or token-budget packing. Added non-duplicate implementation slices: pm-3hps (derived signal feature store), pm-h3no (pluggable SDK scorer contract), and pm-55ra (diversity-aware token-budget packer). Algorithm sketch: build an index-backed candidate set from focus/claim/query/dependency windows; compute normalized signal vectors for recency, activity density, graph proximity, priority/risk/deadline pressure, semantic similarity, knowledge density, author affinity, and continuity deltas; combine them through a deterministic default scorer with extension override; pack by marginal utility per token while preserving required anchors and degrading projection depth before dropping items; expose score breakdowns for explainability and pm-atfm evaluation.

author: codex-agent
message: Adaptive context engine planning slices recorded and verified via pm-doxj linked tests

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Algorithm brainstorm 2026-07-07 (planning pass) — concrete candidate algorithms for the scorer, extending the body's signal list with the combination machinery:
(1) Scale-free recency via ACT-R base-level activation: activation(i) = ln(sum over accesses t_j of t_j^-d), d~0.5, computed from the item's history stream timestamps. Log-time decay is the one law that behaves identically for a minutes-old scratch workspace and a decades-old archive (the spectrum invariant pm-z436/pm-g2zz demand) and it rewards frequency AND recency in one term, replacing ad-hoc half-life constants.
(2) Spreading activation for associative recall: seed activation at the focus item, active claims, and current plan step; propagate over typed edges (blocked_by/parent/related plus semantic k-NN edges from the hybrid index) with per-edge-type damping; k iterations of sparse matrix-vector products over the derived read index keeps it O(neighborhood), never O(workspace).
(3) Learned signal weights with explainability preserved: keep the final score a LINEAR combination of the per-signal contributions (so --explain-ranking stays truthful), but fit the weights per workspace offline against the pm-atfm eval harness as the objective (pairwise learning-to-rank on 'agent actually used this item next' labels derivable from history streams). Weights persist in settings; default weights ship for cold start.
(4) Token-budget packing as submodular selection: greedy by marginal-value-per-token (MMR-style redundancy penalty so two near-duplicate items do not both spend budget), with per-item projection degradation levels (full body -> summary -> id+title) as fractional knapsack steps. Greedy is provably near-optimal for submodular objectives and is deterministic.
All four stay SDK-side behind scoreContextCandidates; none require new stored state beyond what history streams and the read index already hold.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Algorithm brainstorm 2026-07-07 (planning pass, continuation of (1)-(4)):
(5) Reciprocal rank fusion as the cold-start combiner: before a workspace has enough history for (3)'s learned linear weights, combining per-signal RANKS instead of scores — score(i) = sum over signals s of 1/(k + rank_s(i)), k~60 — needs no calibration between signals with incompatible scales (activation vs cosine similarity vs priority ordinals), is deterministic, and is robust to any one signal degenerating (a broken/empty signal ranks everything equally and cancels out). Graduation path: ship RRF as the zero-config default, fit (3)'s linear weights per workspace once pm-atfm labels exist, keep RRF as the documented fallback. Explainability holds: per-signal rank contributions are exactly reportable in --explain-ranking.
(6) Write-time incremental signal maintenance: at millions of items (pm-g2zz) even O(candidates) re-scoring at read time is dominated by recomputing per-item activation/affinity from raw history streams. Because every mutation already lands one immutable history entry, fold signal state incrementally at write time: each history append updates that item's running activation term, author-affinity counters, and semantic-neighborhood invalidation in the derived read index (pm-801d) — an event-sourced materialized view of the scorer inputs. Read-time work drops to combine-and-pack (4); rebuild-from-history stays the recovery path, preserving the derived-never-authoritative invariant. This is also the natural home for the per-author watermarks that pm-vvib candidates #4 novelty and #5 theory-of-mind derive — one fold pass maintains all of them.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Algorithm brainstorm 2026-07-07 (planning pass, continuation of (1)-(6)):
(7) Two-stage retrieval funnel with per-source candidate quotas — (1)-(6) specify how to SCORE and PACK, but at millions of items (pm-g2zz) the scorer must never see the whole workspace; the missing explicit piece is the recall stage of the classic recall-then-rank architecture. Candidate generation unions bounded top-k slates from independent cheap sources, each with its own quota: (a) ANN top-k from the hybrid semantic index, (b) n-hop graph expansion from focus/claims/plan steps (the seed set of (2)), (c) top-k by folded activation from the write-time view of (6) (a simple sorted structure makes this O(k)), (d) the agent's own WIP/claimed set and watermark-delta (novelty candidates), (e) exact lexical/field-predicate hits. Per-source quotas guarantee slate DIVERSITY structurally (no single degenerate signal floods the pool) and make cost independent of workspace size: scoring is O(sum of quotas), never O(items). Quotas scale with the token budget, which is how the same funnel serves a 3-item scratch workspace (every source returns everything, union is the whole workspace) and a decades-deep archive unchanged — the spectrum invariant. Explainability extends naturally: --explain-ranking reports which source(s) recalled each candidate before the per-signal score breakdown. SDK-side behind scoreContextCandidates like (1)-(6); composes directly with (5) RRF, which can fuse the per-source ranks as its cold-start combiner.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Algorithm brainstorm 2026-07-07 (planning pass, continuation of (1)-(7)):
(8) Sketch-backed scorer state for million-item scale — (6)'s write-time fold keeps per-item activation and per-author affinity counters, but at the pm-g2zz extreme (millions of items x thousands of concurrent authors) exact per-(author, item/tag) counter matrices become the new scale bottleneck: state grows O(authors x touched-items) and dominates the derived read index. Replace exact counters with probabilistic sketches, each with provable error bounds: (a) Count-Min sketch for author-affinity and tag/field co-occurrence counts (sub-linear memory, point-query overestimate bounded by epsilon*N with tunable width/depth); (b) HyperLogLog for distinct-cardinality signals (how many distinct authors/sessions touched this subtree — a trust/centrality input) at ~1.5KB per counter regardless of cardinality; (c) exponential-decay variants (time-decayed CM sketch) so ACT-R-style recency folding from (1) composes directly — decay applied at fold time, no rescan. Spectrum-neutral by construction: below a size threshold the fold stores EXACT counters and the sketch path is dormant; the switchover is an internal representation change invisible to --explain-ranking consumers (signal semantics identical, only precision epsilon differs, and the envelope can disclose 'approximate' when sketched). Derived-never-authoritative holds: sketches are read-index state, rebuildable from immutable history streams at any time; error compounds only within a rebuild epoch. Composes with (7)'s funnel — per-source top-k slates read sketch summaries, never raw streams — and with (5) RRF, which consumes ranks and is inherently robust to bounded count error. SDK-side behind scoreContextCandidates like (1)-(7).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Algorithm brainstorm 2026-07-07 (planning pass, continuation of (1)-(8)):
(9) Granularity-adaptive packing — coarse-to-fine representation selection under one token budget. (4) treats each candidate as an atomic (gain, cost) pair, but every item admits MULTIPLE representations with monotone cost/value: id-line (~10 tokens) < compact row (~25) < row+AC/deps (~80) < full body (~hundreds), and a PARENT chunk rollup can stand in for an entire sibling subtree (pm-vvib candidate (10) chunking). Formulate packing as a multiple-choice knapsack: for each candidate (or subtree) pick exactly ONE representation level or drop it. Per-level value curves are concave (diminishing returns per token), so the greedy density heuristic of (4) extends cleanly: start with every survivor at its cheapest level, compute marginal value-per-token for each LEVEL UPGRADE, and greedily buy upgrades until the budget is spent. Properties: (a) no cliff — an item that flat packing would cut entirely degrades to its id-line instead, so the agent at least knows it exists (the human gist-memory analog: recall THAT before recall WHAT); (b) chunk substitution is just another level priced at the subtree's rollup size, giving structure-aware compression for free; (c) explainability (3) is preserved — each served row can carry its level plus why it was not upgraded (budget exhausted vs low marginal density). Composes with the funnel (7): recall produces the slate, (1)-(5) value it, granularity packing spends the budget; rollup sizes/digests are maintained by the write-time fold (6) so packing stays read-cheap. Spectrum behavior: small workspaces have budget exceeding full-granularity cost, output identical to today; million-item workspaces emit a few full-detail items + chunk digests + an id-line gist tail, in O(slate x levels, log-factor for the upgrade heap) time.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Algorithm (10) structural fingerprints for analogical recall (Weisfeiler-Leman relational hashing). Serves pm-vvib candidate subsystem (11): retrieve past episodes whose relational SHAPE matches the current situation, independent of vocabulary. Index side: for every item, extract its typed local neighborhood (parent/child edges, dep kinds, status-transition sequence from history, close_reason class, link types) and run k rounds of WL label refinement — each round rehashes an item label with the multiset of its neighbors' labels, so after k rounds the label encodes the k-hop relational shape. The resulting multiset of subtree hashes is the item's structural fingerprint. Similarity = weighted Jaccard over fingerprints; MinHash + LSH banding gives sublinear candidate lookup, so analogical recall is one more bounded-quota source in the two-stage funnel (7), fused via RRF (5) with semantic and associative channels. Costs compose with the existing plan: fingerprints fold incrementally at write time (6) — a mutation re-refines only the k-hop neighborhood it touched; sketch-backed state (8) bounds index size at million-item scale; packing (4)/(9) treats a retrieved precedent as one candidate whose payload is the episode digest (resolution + top learning), never the raw subgraph. Explainability preserved (3): the matched WL subtree hashes decode to which relations aligned ("both had a wontfix blocker feeding a release-gated dependent"), rendered as one line in --explain-ranking. Spectrum behavior: small workspaces have few distinct shapes so candidates collapse into recency/semantic ranking (signal silent, zero cost); decades-scale workspaces get case-based precedent recall no content signal can produce. Distinct from (2) spreading activation (connectivity along EXPLICIT shared edges; this matches shape between DISCONNECTED regions) and from embedding similarity (content, not structure).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Algorithm (11) open-loop tension scoring with decay floors (Zeigarnik persistence). Serves pm-vvib candidate subsystem (12): incomplete work must persist in assembled context until completed or deliberately parked, never fade under recency decay. Mechanism: maintain a per-agent open-loop registry — folded incrementally at write time (6) like all derived state — holding every loop the agent owns: claimed items, Decisions without recorded resolution, notes ending in unanswered questions, reviews awaiting reply. Each loop carries a tension score composed of deadline proximity, blocker fan-in (how many items the loop blocks — consumes the dep graph), decision-pending markers, and a claim-age shape that rises then plateaus, so ancient abandoned WIP hands off to stale-WIP hygiene (pm-w8q4) instead of dominating forever. Tension acts as a FLOOR under the salience scorer's decay term (1): an open loop's effective salience never drops below its tension, so it cannot be evicted by token-budget packing (4)/(9) while live. Release events — close, claim release, explicit parking (blocked-with-reason) — zero the floor and return the item to ordinary consolidation-managed scoring; each release is itself an entry in the immutable history stream, so the registry rebuilds exactly from replay. Bounded by design: the registry is capped per agent at working-memory scale (~7 loops, composing with chunking (9): overflow loops collapse under their parent chunk), so cost is O(active agents), independent of workspace size; sketch-backed state (8) is unnecessary here because the set is intrinsically small. Explainability (3): floored items render one line in --explain-ranking — held: open loop, claimed 3d ago, blocks 2, decision pending. Spectrum behavior: small workspaces never hit packing limits so the floor is a silent no-op; million-item thousand-agent workspaces guarantee every agent resumes with its own unfinished business first at zero scan cost. Distinct from (2) spreading activation (cue-driven associative boost; this is cue-INDEPENDENT persistence) and from (7) funnel quotas (source selection before fusion; this is a rank floor after fusion).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Algorithm (12) co-change coupling mining + outcome-conditioned precedent projection (episodic future thinking; serves pm-vvib candidate 13). Two composable parts. Part A — evolutionary coupling from episodic streams: mine 'items/files that change together' association statistics directly from the immutable history (mining-software-repositories co-change coupling): for item pairs (i,j), maintain support = |sessions touching both| and confidence(i→j) = support / |sessions touching i|, where a 'session' is a same-author time-windowed burst of mutations (or an explicit focus session). Computed as a WRITE-TIME FOLD (algorithm 6) into a count-min-sketch-backed pair table (algorithm 8) so cost is O(burst width) per mutation and storage stays sublinear at millions of items; decay counts with ACT-R log-time activation (algorithm 1) so decades-old coupling fades without a cliff. This yields the tracker's latent 'hidden dependency' graph — edges nobody declared but history proves, exactly the edges that predict surprise breakage. Part B — outcome-conditioned precedent projection: given a target item (about to be claimed / planned), retrieve top-k precedents by WL structural fingerprint (algorithm 10) UNIONED with top-k by co-change neighborhood overlap from Part A, then score each precedent by outcome relevance: divergence signals from its close record (expected_result vs actual_result mismatch, reopen events, close_reason class, time-to-close vs estimate from pm-3hps calibration data). Emit (a) predicted blast radius = co-change neighbors above a confidence floor that are NOT in the declared dep/parent/file neighborhood (candidate advisory edges, feeding the pm-4ri6 dedupe/advisory surface), and (b) top failure precedents with one-line 'how it actually ended' summaries for the pm context / pm plan pre-mortem block. Both parts are recall-stage sources for the two-stage funnel (algorithm 7, new quota source) and their contributions stay linear/explainable in the scorer (algorithm 3). Spectrum-invariant: sketch + log decay for the millions-of-items end (pm-g2zz), degenerates gracefully to exact small tables under the pm-z436 small-end profile.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Algorithm (13) salience-gated broadcast arbitration with habituation (global-workspace competition; serves pm-vvib candidate 14). Purpose: in a workspace with thousands of concurrent agents, decide WHICH few events earn workspace-wide attention and for HOW LONG, without a daemon and without flooding every context envelope. Mechanism, three stages. Stage 1 — local salience scoring at write time: every mutation event already folds derived state (algorithm 6); the same fold computes a broadcast score as an explainable linear combination (algorithm 3) of: surprise (inverse of the event's predicted probability given co-change statistics, algorithm 12 Part A — an edit nobody's coupling history predicted scores high), blast radius (count of items/agents whose open loops (11) or claimed WIP sit inside the event's co-change + dep neighborhood), outcome severity (negativity-weighted: failure/wontfix/regression close-reasons outrank routine closes, pm-vvib candidate 7), and deadline pressure (proximity of release-gated dependents). Stage 2 — bounded workspace arbitration: a fixed-capacity broadcast set (~4 slots, human working-memory scale) maintained as a fold over the event stream (pm-e200 substrate, ADR pm-i7i4 no-daemon: arbitration re-evaluates lazily whenever any agent next assembles context); an event enters only by exceeding the weakest incumbent, and incumbents decay by ACT-R log-time activation (1) so the channel self-clears. Stage 3 — habituation / inhibition-of-return: repeated structurally similar events (same WL fingerprint class, algorithm 10) attenuate geometrically so one flaky gate cannot monopolize a slot, and an agent's acknowledgment (an ordinary immutable history event) suppresses re-serving THAT event to THAT agent while leaving it live for others — per-agent delivery state is derivable from each agent's own episodic stream, zero new authoritative state. Costs: O(1) score per mutation amortized via the existing fold; arbitration is O(slots) per context assembly; sketch-backed fingerprint classes (8) bound habituation memory at million-item scale. Explainability (3): each broadcast slot renders one line with its winning terms ("broadcast: wontfix close blocks 3 release-gated items, 2 agents in radius"). Spectrum behavior: single-agent or low-traffic workspaces never fill slot 1 above threshold — the block is absent and costs nothing; thousand-agent workspaces get coherent shared attention (no duplicated discovery, no missed cross-lane hazards) for ~4 lines of tokens per envelope. Distinct from (2) spreading activation (cue-driven, per-focus recall boost; this is cue-independent workspace-wide push), from (11) open-loop floors (persistence of MY unfinished work; this is arbitration among EVERYONE's events), and from the retrieval funnel (7) quotas (which select candidate items per source; this selects events for a dedicated always-on attention block).

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-08 (third follow-up): Algorithm (14) retrieval-conditioned contradiction gating for memory reconsolidation (serves pm-vvib candidate 15). Purpose: decide WHEN a retrieved precedent becomes eligible for an append-only correction delta, so the corpus self-corrects under use without thrashing on every read. Mechanism, three stages. Stage 1 — retrieval events as cheap immutable facts: whenever context assembly (algorithm 7 funnel) serves a precedent item into an agent's envelope, fold a (retriever, precedent_id, ts) retrieval event into sketch state (algorithm 8) — O(1), no daemon, replayable from the streams. Stage 2 — contradiction scoring at the NEXT write: when the retrieving agent later records an outcome on its own item (close with resolution/expected/actual, a learning, a decision), compute an explainable linear contradiction score (algorithm 3 weights) between that outcome and each recently-retrieved precedent: (a) outcome divergence — precedent's recorded resolution vs the new expected-vs-actual pair (categorical mismatch = strong signal); (b) estimate divergence — |log(actual_minutes/estimated_minutes)| of the new work when the precedent supplied the estimate prior; (c) semantic opposition — embedding distance flip between precedent decision text and the new learning text (reuses the existing vector index, no new store); (d) structural relevance gate — WL fingerprint similarity (algorithm 10) between the two work contexts, so only contradictions in STRUCTURALLY SIMILAR situations count (a divergent outcome in an unrelated context is noise, not correction pressure). Eligibility opens only when score crosses a threshold AND the habituation guard passes: per (retriever, precedent) pair at most one open window per K retrievals, mirroring the biological constraint that reconsolidation needs a labile window, not constant rewriting. Stage 3 — correction pressure as a fold-time retrieval signal: accepted deltas append as ordinary immutable events pairing (precedent_id, retrieving_context_id, correction); write-time maintenance (algorithm 6) folds a decaying correction-pressure counter per precedent that (i) demotes the precedent's retrieval rank proportionally, floored so originals never disappear (immutability + auditability), and (ii) attaches the N most recent corrections whenever the precedent IS served (candidate 15 acceptance shape). Confirmations (retrievals followed by consistent outcomes) fold the opposite way and harden rank — spaced retrieval-practice strengthening (pm-uwfs territory) composes here rather than duplicating. Spectrum behavior: small workspaces rarely cross threshold, cost is one sketch fold per retrieval; million-item workspaces get a queryable relitigation surface — precedents with sustained correction pressure are exactly the decisions the organisation keeps re-arguing, surfaced via ordinary list/search over folded metadata rather than a batch job.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-09 (fourth follow-up): Algorithm (15) age-and-value-conditioned verbatim decay with gist-preserving compaction (serves pm-vvib candidate 16 fuzzy-trace dual representation). Purpose: decide WHICH items and WHEN to compress from verbatim to gist so the CORPUS itself stays token-cheap to hold, without ever destroying verbatim or breaking replay — the storage-side dual of read-time packing (9), which selects representation per QUERY. Distinct from (9): (9) chooses a level to SERVE and leaves bytes untouched; (15) chooses bytes to MOVE behind a replayable checkpoint so the default-served layer is smaller for everyone, permanently. Mechanism, three stages. Stage 1 — eligibility scoring (explainable linear form, algorithm 3 weights), computed only for CLOSED, quiescent items so live/open work is never compacted: (a) age since closed_at (needs pm-m4iu; until then falls back to last-history-event ts, never updated_at — the drag-trap field is banned here); (b) quiescence — no reads/retrievals (algorithm 8 sketch retrieval counters from candidate-15 stage 1) and no reconsolidation corrections (candidate 15) in the last K epochs, so precedents under active relitigation stay full-fidelity; (c) verbatim mass — bytes recoverable by compaction (long bodies/comment threads/raw history) so effort targets high-yield items; (d) NEGATIVE weight for structural centrality — items that are frequent analogical precedents (algorithm 10 WL fingerprint hub-ness) or high co-change hubs (algorithm 12) keep verbatim longer because their detail is repeatedly reused. Stage 2 — gist synthesis with provenance: for an eligible item, fold a durable gist digest from the fields fuzzy-trace theory says survive — title, close_reason, resolution, expected/actual, top structural edges, and a bounded extractive summary of the body — and stamp it with a provenance pointer to the exact history-compact checkpoint the verbatim was moved behind. The gist is itself an immutable derived event (write-time fold, algorithm 6); verbatim bytes are relocated by history-compact, NOT deleted, so get --at <version> reconstructs full verbatim on demand and the hash chain still verifies (history-compact's existing replay-integrity guarantee is the substrate; this algorithm only supplies the scheduling policy and the gist payload). Stage 3 — divergence guard (the false-memory detector candidate 16 requires): whenever verbatim is later reconstructed (an agent expands an item), cheaply re-derive the gist from the reconstructed verbatim and compare to the stored gist digest; a mismatch beyond tolerance means the gist drifted from its source (bad summary, or a reconsolidation correction that should have updated the gist but did not) and is surfaced as a validate/health signal keyed to the provenance pointer — never silently trusted. Un-provenanced gist (a digest with no checkpoint pointer) is likewise a hard validate finding. Composes: retrieval funnel (7) serves gist by default; packing (9) prices verbatim expansion as the top representation level; reconsolidation corrections (algorithm 14) attach at the gist layer so they survive compaction; chunking (algorithm 9 rollups) can pack a parent's gist over children's gists for structure-aware compression of compressed items. Spectrum behavior: small/young workspaces have no closed-and-quiescent items so eligibility never fires — one cheap fold per close, zero compaction, full verbatim always; million-item multi-decade workspaces converge to a working set of full-verbatim live/central items plus a long tail of gist-only closed items at a fraction of the bytes, each expandable and hash-verifiable on demand. Cost: O(closed-and-newly-quiescent items per epoch) for eligibility, all folded at write time / gc time (no daemon, ADR pm-i7i4); divergence checks are pay-on-expand, O(1) per reconstruction.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-09 (fifth follow-up): Algorithm (16) successor-representation predictive map over agent trajectories (serves pm-vvib candidate 17 cognitive map). Purpose: give agents ORIENTATION — region, landmarks, goal direction, latent shortcuts — as folded derived state, the computational account of hippocampal cognitive maps (Dayan's successor representation; Stachenfeld et al.: place cells encode expected discounted future state occupancy, grid cells are the SR's eigenvectors). Mechanism, three stages. Stage 1 — trajectory extraction: each agent's episodic stream already records the ordered sequence of items it touches (mutations, plus retrieval events from algorithm 14 stage 1); fold same-author time-windowed transitions i→j at write time (algorithm 6), O(1) per mutation, replayable from the immutable streams. Stage 2 — SR maintenance: maintain M(i,j) ≈ expected discounted future occupancy of j given presence at i via incremental TD(λ)-style updates per folded transition; at scale, never hold the full matrix — keep a top-d spectral embedding (the "grid-cell" basis) with sketch-backed state (algorithm 8), so storage is O(items × d) and updates stay local; ACT-R log-time decay (algorithm 1) ages transitions so decade-old traffic patterns fade without cliffs. Stage 3 — map services, all read-time and bounded: (a) ORIENTATION — regions emerge as clusters in SR-embedding space (traversal-defined, complementing declared-hierarchy chunking in algorithm 9); landmarks = highest stationary-occupancy hubs (milestone/epics/ADRs surface naturally); the pm context orientation block renders focus item's region, nearest landmarks with hop counts, and expected-steps-to-goal. (b) GOAL-CONDITIONED DISTANCE — expected traversal steps between focus and the agent's claimed milestone read directly off the SR, a principled next-step input to pm next's ranking (which item moves me toward my goal, not just which is globally ready). (c) SHORTCUT INFERENCE — high SR similarity between items/regions with NO declared edge and NO co-change support = a latent route worth surfacing as an advisory edge (pm-4ri6 surface); complements algorithm 12 co-change (pairwise same-burst coupling) because SR composes MULTI-STEP discounted paths — it predicts where work LEADS, not just what it touches together. (d) ZOOM — coarse SR eigenvectors give large-scale regions, finer ones give local structure, exactly the multi-scale levels candidate 17 serves; packing (algorithms 4/9) prices each zoom level like any representation level. Explainability (3): every orientation line decodes to its occupancy statistics ("region: release-tooling — you touched 7 of its items this week; landmark pm-doxj, 2 hops; goal ~4 steps via pm-wrbe"). Distinct from (2) spreading activation (one-shot cue diffusion at read time over explicit edges; SR is a LEARNED long-horizon expectation folded at write time), from (12) co-change mining (pairwise burst statistics; SR is sequential, discounted, and composes routes), and from (10) WL fingerprints (static structural shape; SR is dynamic traversal statistics — two identically-shaped subgraphs can occupy opposite ends of the traffic map). Spectrum behavior: small/young workspaces — transitions are too sparse for regions to form, SR stays near-diagonal, orientation block suppressed below threshold, cost one fold per mutation; million-item thousand-agent workspaces — the map is the only sublinear way for a cold agent to orient and route without scans, and per-agent trajectories fold independently so concurrency needs no coordination beyond the existing append-only streams.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Planning pass 2026-07-09 (sixth follow-up): Algorithm (17) learning-progress-guided gap scheduling — an epistemic-foraging queue (serves pm-vvib candidate 18 curiosity drive). Purpose: rank what the workspace should LEARN next, so exploration (filling knowledge gaps) gets a principled, bounded share of agent attention alongside exploitation (pm next's ready work), using only signals that already exist. Mechanism, three stages. Stage 1 — gap-signal folding at write time (algorithm 6): maintain per-item and per-region (regions from algorithm 16 SR clusters, falling back to parent subtree via algorithm 9 chunking when the SR is too sparse) an uncertainty vector with four families: (a) completeness — count of absent high-value fields (AC, risk, confidence, resolution triple, close_reason), weighted by the item's retrieval frequency (algorithm 14 stage-1 sketch counters) so gaps in items agents actually USE outrank gaps in dead corners; (b) prediction residuals — per-region rolling error of the predictive layers (co-change algorithm 12: predicted-but-untouched and touched-but-unpredicted; SR algorithm 16: realized-trajectory divergence), folded as decaying counters (algorithm 1); (c) reliability blindness — retrieval-without-outcome ratio per precedent (served k times, zero confirmations/corrections from algorithm 14 stage 2), i.e. trust with no evidence; (d) isolation — hybrid-index neighborhood sparsity below a floor (no near neighbors = unreachable by association = invisible to spreading activation (2)). All sketch-backed (algorithm 8), O(1) amortized per mutation, replayable, no daemon (ADR pm-i7i4). Stage 2 — learning-progress estimator (the curiosity core): per region, track the DERIVATIVE of each error family over fold epochs; score curiosity as an explainable linear combination (algorithm 3) of gap magnitude × |error slope|, gated by the inverted-U: suppress mastered regions (error low and flat — nothing to learn) and unlearnable ones (error high and flat for M epochs — more sampling will not help; typically means missing instrumentation or genuinely chaotic territory, flagged once to validate instead of burning agent tokens). This is Oudeyer-style learning progress applied to a work corpus: attention flows to where the map is improvably wrong, and automatically withdraws as a region is either mastered or written off. Stage 3 — bounded exploration serving: the gap queue becomes one additional quota source in the retrieval funnel (algorithm 7) and an opt-in section of pm next — at most ONE gap-fill suggestion per envelope, rendered with its winning terms ("gap: release-tooling region — 12 retrieved-without-confirmation precedents, prediction error fell 40% last epoch, top item pm-XXXX missing risk+AC"), each suggestion a concrete enrichment action (add the missing field, confirm/correct the precedent, link the isolated item). Serving folds a curiosity-visit event so the same gap is not re-served while an agent works it (habituation, algorithm 13 stage 3 pattern; inhibition-of-return per agent derivable from its own stream). Explicitly NOT a new item generator by default: suggestions are actions on EXISTING items — item creation stays a human/agent decision, keeping the no-duplicates invariant intact. Distinct from (13) broadcast arbitration (pushes currently-salient EVENTS; this pulls toward stable KNOWLEDGE deficits), from (14) contradiction gating (corrects precedents PROVEN wrong; this targets precedents whose reliability is UNKNOWN), and from validate (static rule findings at a point in time; this is a dynamic, usage-weighted, progress-gated ranking over those findings plus model residuals). Spectrum behavior: small/young workspaces — error series too short, inverted-U gate keeps the quota empty, cost one fold per mutation; million-item decades-old workspaces — the corpus self-heals where it is used most, and the unlearnable-region flag gives maintainers a map of where instrumentation (not effort) is the missing ingredient. Completes the exploit/explore pair: algorithms 1–16 optimize serving what is known; 17 allocates attention to what is not.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text:
Synthesis 2026-07-09 (planning pass): dependency ordering of algorithms (1)-(17) onto the four existing implementation slices, replacing further territory scouting (catalog complete at 17).

OWNERSHIP MAP — every algorithm has exactly one owning slice:
- pm-h3no (scorer contract): (5) RRF cold-start combiner ships as the zero-config default combiner inside the contract; (3) learned linear weights is the contract's documented graduation path (needs pm-uwfs labels + pm-atfm objective); (1) ACT-R activation defines the contract's canonical signal shape.
- pm-3hps (signal feature store): (1) activation computation, (2) spreading activation over typed edges, (6) write-time incremental fold (event-sourced scorer state; also home of the per-author watermarks candidates #4/#5 need), (8) sketch-backed state at million-item scale, and the mined signals (10) WL fingerprints, (12) co-change coupling, (16) successor-representation map as late additions.
- pm-55ra (packer): (4) submodular greedy packing, (9) granularity-adaptive coarse-to-fine projection, (7) two-stage retrieval funnel with per-source quotas (the packer's candidate-supply side; also the quota surface (17) plugs into).
- pm-uwfs (usage feedback): label source for (3) and the learning-progress denominator for (17).
Lifecycle/governance algorithms (11) tension floors, (13) broadcast arbitration, (14) contradiction gating, (15) gist compaction, (17) gap scheduling stay ADR-gated (pm-vvib) — they get owning items only on acceptance, wired to the fold (6) and funnel (7) surfaces.

WAVE ORDER (encoded in the graph today: pm-3hps and pm-55ra now blocked_by pm-h3no):
- Wave 1 — first implementation slice, read-time only, zero new stored state: contract (pm-h3no) with (1)+(5)+(4): activation from history timestamps, RRF combination, greedy budget packing. Deterministic, explainable, small-workspace neutral — directly testable against the pm-4k6b AC golden tests.
- Wave 2 — retrieval scale: (7) funnel + (2) spreading activation + (9) granularity packing; needs pm-801d read-index edges and pm-dfg0 pagination.
- Wave 3 — write-time state: (6) fold, then (8) sketches; unlocks novelty/theory-of-mind watermarks and (11)/(13) counters.
- Wave 4 — mined and learned: (3) weights, (10), (12), (16); all consume wave-3 fold infrastructure plus pm-atfm/pm-uwfs.
- Wave 5 — lifecycle governance: (14), (15), (17); gated on pm-vvib acceptance and waves 2-3 surfaces.

Recommendation: wave 1 is ADR-independent and unblocked once pm-rjqr/pm-x6jf SDK promotion lands — it is the natural next engineering start on this epic.
